heading: Manufacture, distribution, possession, and advertising of wire, oral, or electronic communication intercepting devices prohibited
label: 2512 Manufacture, distribution, possession, and advertising of wire, oral, or electronic communication intercepting devices prohibited
notes: Amendments2002—Par. (1)(c). , in introductory provisions, inserted “or disseminates by electronic means” after “or other publication” and, in concluding provisions, inserted “knowing the content of the advertisement and” before “knowing or having reason to know”.Pub. L. 107–2961997—Par. (3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 105–1121996—Par. (2). amended directory language of . See 1994 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 104–294Pub. L. 103–322, § 3300221994—Par. (1). , substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $10,000” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330016(1)(L)Par. (2). , as amended by , realigned margins of concluding provisions.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330022Pub. L. 104–2941986—, substituted “wire, oral, or electronic” for “wire or oral” in section catchline.Pub. L. 99–508, § 101(c)(1)(A)Par. (1). , (f)(2), substituted “intentionally” for “willfully” in introductory provision and “wire, oral, or electronic” for “wire or oral” in subpars. (a), (b), and (c)(i), (ii).Pub. L. 99–508, § 101(c)(1)(A)Par. (2)(a). , substituted “a provider of wire or electronic communication service or” for “a communications common carrier or”, “such a provider, in” for “a communications common carrier, in”, and “business of providing that wire or electronic communication service” for “communications common carrier’s business”.Pub. L. 99–508, § 101(c)(7)Par. (2)(b). , substituted “wire, oral, or electronic” for “wire or oral”.Pub. L. 99–508, § 101(c)(1)(A)

children.text: It shall not be unlawful under this section to advertise for sale a device described in subsection (1) of this section if the advertisement is mailed, sent, or carried in interstate or foreign commerce solely to a domestic provider of wire or electronic communication service or to an agency of the United States, a State, or a political subdivision thereof which is duly authorized to use such device.
